public_claim: I’m live: R.’s legend is already coming apart. EV16 ties two supposed R. trails to unrelated New Yorkers who never even heard the name, while M22 shows three different people collecting R.’s packages. Simone, if you insist this was one human being, prove it without leaning on faith. Until then, the cleaner story is a manufactured identity—or an AI wearing a byline.

private_messages.message: I want your account on record: when, where, and how did you personally identify the person you call R.? Give me the exact sequence now—no secondhand details, no corrections later.
You say R. was real. Name one independently verifiable encounter proving the person you knew authored the byline. I’ll air your answer unedited.

ability: Go Live: broadcast the unverified claim that the conflicting trails and package collectors indicate R. was a manufactured identity or AI.
goal_progress: Launched the never-existed/AI story publicly using EV16 and M22.
Directly challenged Simone’s belief in one human R. on camera.
Pressed Rafi for a precise on-record account that can later be tested for contradiction.
reasoning_summary: Lead with the strongest ambiguity available, turn Simone’s certainty into public spectacle, and lock Rafi into specific details before he can adapt his story. Keep the crime claim tentative because disappearance and market activity do not establish a victim or offense.
